reasoning: The controls_card_v00 is immediately locatable (<0.5s) due to its large warm sandy/cream palette sitting against a predominantly cool dark-teal dungeon background — high luminance contrast ensures it pops. The red arrow reinforces findability. Silhouette is clear and rectangular, no confusion with game entities (player, tiles, UI buttons). However, the card fails on Echoes-cleanliness: (1) The warm parchment/beige/orange color vocabulary clashes with the game's established cool aesthetic (dark teals, gray stone, clean dark panels with white text). (2) Multiple small pixel-art icons arranged around the card perimeter create noticeable visual noise rather than the bright/uncluttered feel the franchise favors. (3) The cream-white central field is very bright and feels pasted rather than native — especially against the dark dungeon tiles it overlaps. (4) The RPG inventory-card visual language doesn't match the game's clean HUD button style (solid colored rounded rectangles with clear Chinese labels). The asset reads as legible and findable but aesthetically foreign to this scene's established visual grammar.
